seeded_from: extraction-loop-design.md section 7, in the priority order it gives
selection_rule: highest expected field yield first, ties broken by lowest attempt count (design section C). With this seed that means T1 first.
note: Every `fields` list below was re-derived from loop/baseline.json's field_state on day 0, not copied from the design. All seven agree with design section 0.1 exactly: 5 ca_dlse abstains, 5 external abstains, 2 il_dol blocked, 5 masked, 22 image-only (hi_ags 6, ou 7, osu 3, lcc 6).

items.title: ca_dlse_hourly: the watermark filter deletes the whole page (malformed FontBBox sizes)
items.root_cause_note: read_words returns 0 words for a page on which raw pdfplumber sees 96 words / 630 chars. The PDF's font descriptor has a malformed FontBBox, so every char on the page reports a bogus size of 27.3-48.6pt, and read_words filters size >= WATERMARK_MIN_SIZE (20.0) as watermark. The extractor is blind to the entire page; nothing downstream ever runs.
items.design_note: Design section 8.1: ONE unit of work, not five. Five partial fixes around a blind reader are exactly the page-specific patching constraint 2 forbids. Candidate licenses for P2 to weigh: a physical bound (a 'watermark' classification that removes EVERY char on a page is self-refuting -- a watermark is by definition distinguishable from the body it overlays) or measured glyph-box height in place of declared font size. The falsification sweep must additionally prove real >=20pt content elsewhere in the corpus (pack headings, actual watermarks) is still handled -- that is the named hazard. The proposal must still predict all five flips individually; G7 holds it to that. It must also predict that this document's two masked-year fields (pay_period_start/end) stay abstained -- a flip there would mean the change invented a century, and is a G7 anomaly.
items.design_risk: Design section 9.2: T1 changes how every page is read, so predicting zero incidental flips across 77 documents is a strong claim. Expect one REVIEW round-trip here; it is the likeliest place the loop burns its second expensive call.
items.closed_note: 3 of 5 fields recovered (regular_hours, gross_pay, net_pay). person_name and hourly_rate were measured to stay abstain with the page readable: they sit under a 29pt non-bold header row the header path does not recognise. That is a label/geometry cause, not reader loss, so it is a separate target rather than a patch inside this one.

items.title: ext_les pay_period_start: printed compound label `Pay Begin Date`
items.root_cause_note: Printed verbatim in the text layer as `Pay Begin Date 7/22/2010`. A printed label is license class 1 and the label is not in the vocabulary.
items.design_note: Likely a label-vocabulary addition. Falsify the string against all 77 before implementing.

items.title: ext_unc regular_hours: row word plus header columns
items.root_cause_note: Printed as `Regular 20.346846 74.50 1,515.84 855.00 17,446.65 ...` -- a row word under printed header columns.
items.design_note: Column-path diagnosis; may already be near the committed core/columns.py capability.

items.title: external person_name x3: names printed with no adjacent label
items.root_cause_note: ADP: `Taxable Marital Status: Single AMIS SMITH`, the name floats over the address block with no name label adjacent. UTEP: `Mary Doe 55555 ABC Company 09-30-2014 ...`, a dense unlabelled row. LES: `STALLONE, SYLVESTER GS 07 05 $44,616.00 ...`.
items.design_note: LES may have a printed NAME header cell; the other two are probable NO_SAFE_RULE. Say so early rather than burn attempts -- one P2 look each, closed on the first NO_SAFE_RULE. Design section 9.3: the loop is expected to spend most of its life writing NO_SAFE_RULE records, and closing items is progress.

items.title: il_dol two date fields: two-digit years the frozen scorer cannot represent
items.root_cause_note: Blocked twice. (a) The output contract emits ISO dates and commit 2bb7337 deliberately refuses two-digit years. (b) Independently and absolutely: eval/score_extraction._as_date has no two-digit-year pattern, so truth `8/27/05` normalizes to ('date', UNPARSABLE, '8/27/05'). An extractor emitting the correct `2005-08-27` would be scored WRONG. The only string that scores correct is the literal `8/27/05`, which the ISO contract forbids emitting. Verified independently on day 0: both fields classify as blocked_contract in field_state by exactly that test.
items.design_note: Design section 8.2: a span-borrow license DOES exist on the page for pay_period_end -- `Pay Period: 8/21/2005 to 8/27/05` prints the century on the same line inside the same span construct, and a rule borrowing A's century for B when B's two digits equal A's last two invents no digit and is refusal-biased. It still does not matter, because the frozen scorer forecloses it. pay_date (`Paydate: 9/2/05`) has no printed century relation at all and is out of reach regardless. Unblocking is an output-contract / scorer decision belonging to the repo owner, not to any extraction rule. Record in STOP.md; spend nothing.

items.title: five masked-year fields: no century or decade digit is printed anywhere
items.root_cause_note: Printed `1/7/XX`, `1/13/XX`, `1/20/XX`. Any emitted ISO date invents at least two digits -- constraint 3, no argument available.
items.design_note: Design section 8.2: the only conceivable honest treatment is an output-contract extension for partial dates, which measure_confirm_set._masked_date could then score. Changing the contract is not this loop's decision. STOP.md records the option; the loop does not act on it.

items.title: 22 image-only fields in four hybrid documents
fields: see loop/baseline.json field_state, the 22 confirm entries valued image_only
items.root_cause_note: The four documents are hybrid, not scans: they carry large text layers (2.3k-10.3k chars of explanatory prose) with the actual pay stub embedded as a raster image. The 22 values exist only in those pixels. Measured image resolutions: hi_ags/ou/osu ~150-200 dpi (OCR-viable), lcc ~96-116 dpi (marginal).
items.design_note: Design section 8.3 gives the five go/no-go inputs a future OCR effort must obtain before starting -- root cause of the existing 0/7, the recoverability upper bound measured offline, a verification story compatible with zero-wrong (do the embedded stubs print closable identities?), the bbox mapping from image pixels to PDF points at IoU>0.5, and the determinism cost of a nondeterministic dependency. Decision rule: start only if (1) is understood, (2) is >= ~two-thirds clean and (3) closes identities on at least the ou/osu-class documents. Otherwise IMAGE_ONLY is the honest boundary of a text-layer extractor.
items.superseded_note: 2026-07-20: ocr-extension-design.md obtained all five inputs and its decision rule said GO, so this closure is superseded by T9-T14. Corrected accounting: of the nominal image-only population, these 22 are loop-visible; 10 carry a closable printed identity (licensable), the other 12 (10 dates, 2 names) have none and are recorded NO_SAFE_RULE at T13. The uploads-31 harness block is recorded at T14.

items.title: ca_dlse_hourly: header row at 29pt non-bold is not recognised
items.root_cause_note: CORRECTED during it-002 diagnosis -- the it-001 note blamed typography, and that is the wrong mechanism. Measured: _label_runs DOES recognise the 29pt non-bold cells (EMPLOYEE via LABEL_MAP, PAY RATE via LABEL_SYNONYMS -- the vocabulary path is typography-independent). What failed was every value rule downstream: the data row sits 39.0pt below the header baseline (781.0 -> 742.0) and VALUE_Y_WINDOW=(6,22) -- fitted to the pack's 14.5-15.3pt pitch -- cannot reach it; the side-by-side and column_value rules read across the row; table_cell_value's REGULAR row sits under a header with no RATE cell; the arithmetic path found 18.00 by label adjacency only and correctly refused it.
items.closed_note: Both fields recovered by columns.header_cell_value (flag REALDOOR_HEADER_CELL, license: printed header row): a vocabulary label that is one whole cell of a printed column-header row (>=3 caption cells, _header_row_words) licenses the single run beneath it within HEADER_SEARCH_BAND that shares x-extent with exactly that cell and no other. Falsified first over all 77 (loop/falsification/it-002.json): fired on 2 docs, zero conflicts. Flips: target person_name='Johnson, Bob' + hourly_rate=18.00, plus uploads::up_024_pay_stub_table.pdf person_name + pay_date -- the fixture authored to probe exactly this layout; its two-data-row columns (regular_hours/hourly_rate/gross_pay) still abstain on the two-candidate refusal, which is the safety property, not a residue: up_024's own manifest says no rule may pick between the two numbers. No successor items.

items.title: OCR injection/reuse (design it-A): embedded-image regions OCR'd into Words for the identity paths only
items.root_cause_note: ocr-extension-design.md section 1: the confirm hybrids never reach OCR at all (text-layer prose routes them down the text path, and the confirm harness only calls core.extract.extract_document), and the existing ocr/ocr_extract.py is page-1-only, LABEL_MAP-only, pack-geometry-fitted. The stub pixels print closable identities (section A: ou four independent closures, osu summary band).
items.design_note: Design section F it-A: region discovery from page.images, crop OCR at the region's own native scale, engine-geometry Words, injection into extract_document behind REALDOOR_OCR_WORDS -- handed ONLY to verified/shredded (the identity-closing paths), never to the label/column value paths. The design's measured floor was +2 (ou hourly_rate, osu gross_pay); the P2 grounding sweep at native scale measured +4 (ou gross/net land in it-A already). Zero wrong under the identity gate everywhere.
items.closed_note: 4 image-only fields read via OCR word injection + the existing identity chain (predicted 4, observed 4). Remaining OCR iterations per the design stay OPEN with their own expected yields.

items.title: OCR band-role completion (design it-B): L4 quantization products, band-role members, L5 hours header
items.root_cause_note: Design sections A.2/A.3: ou regular_hours refuses on S3 two-survivors {68.43, 8.00} (needs the REGULAR row word + hours-column-sums-to-printed-80.00 + L5 unqualified-HOURS header); osu net_pay refuses on band-role assignment (the remaining member of an already-closed band, named by printed row position). Note: it-A's native-scale measurement already landed ou gross/net, which the design had listed here -- this item shrank from 4 to 2 predicted fields.
items.design_note: Design section F it-B, flag REALDOOR_OCR_BAND_ROLE. Blunt review 3: the S3 relaxation is where a wrong value would enter if anywhere; must stay one-distinct-survivor-per-role, and the falsification predicate must fire on ou+osu only.
items.closed_note: Both fields recovered by the band-role completions (flag REALDOOR_OCR_BAND_ROLE, OCR-injected pages only; license: printed total closed by an arithmetic identity). Diagnosis corrections recorded: ou regular_hours refused on THREE hours survivors {3.57, 8.00, 68.43} (backlog said two), resolved by the printed word REGULAR naming the anchored row under a printed header that is exactly HOURS; osu net_pay was hidden by a degenerate product (1666.94 x 1 with a stray form-field '1') putting the band's own line into the earnings exclusion -- the multiplicative identity now testifies to nothing, and the EXISTING band-tail rule emits. L4 from the design's it-B list was subsumed: the frozen product_tolerance had already closed ou's truncated row in it-003, so no tolerance was touched. Falsified over all 77 first: fired on exactly ou+osu, zero conflicts. Predicted 2, observed 2 (loop/measurements/it-004.json).

items.title: OCR labeled total band + instance-conflict guard (design it-C): hi_ags
items.root_cause_note: Design section A.1: the earnings-column total misreads as '2.328.01' (comma->period) so no anchored run forms; the summary band 444.00+372.63+1,511.38=2,328.01 is a labeled ROW (TOTAL GROSS / TOTAL DEDUCTIONS / NET PAY), a form verified.py has no rule for. Three stub instances on the document disagree (p1 truth vs p2 before/after pair); nothing printed selects an instance.
items.design_note: Design section F it-C, flag REALDOOR_OCR_TOTAL_BAND: labeled band via anchor-only strings (shredded _ROW_LABELS precedent) + instance-conflict guard (hazard 5: same-field candidates from different regions that disagree => withdraw all). Best-case only; FED TAXABLE adjacency; one attempt, NO_SAFE_RULE acceptable.

items.title: OCR single-earnings-row band (design it-D): lcc
items.root_cause_note: Design section A.4: identities close on paper (31.00 x 10.51 = 325.81 exact; 325.81 - 61.36 = 264.45; deduction column sums to 61.36) but the earnings table has ONE current-period row, so the >=2-member column-sum guard refuses the anchor. The document's own traps: truth lists regular_hours AND hourly_rate in expect_absent (emitting 31.00/10.51 is a scored WRONG), misread labels at 96 dpi ('TotalPersonalDeductlons'), Total Employer Contributions 113.20 adjacent to the band.
items.design_note: Design section F it-D, flag REALDOOR_OCR_SINGLE_ROW. Highest risk; honest expectation is 'attempt once, accept NO_SAFE_RULE readily'. Expected yield recorded as 0 for that reason; best case 2.

items.title: OCR dates and names: 12 fields with no closable printed identity
items.closed_on: 2026-07-20, zero iterations spent -- recorded from the design dossier (ocr-extension-design.md section C.3), not from a burned attempt
items.root_cause_note: No printed identity closes over free text or a lone date. Candidate licenses weighed and refused in the dossier: label adjacency is the evidence class that shipped person_name='Employer' and the gross_pay=567.00 shape; multi-instance agreement cannot refuse systematic font-level confusion (measured on lcc: the motto and 'Deductlons' misread identically in independent renderings, and Banner ID digit-flips at 96 dpi); shape checks refuse garbage but not plausible wrong names. Dates: the only candidate identity (frequency x period-span) has no printed frequency on any of the four documents, and up_014 proves the corpus itself violates the span convention. A single substituted character is a scored WRONG under G1.
items.design_note: These surface as PROPOSAL-carrying abstentions through verified.py's existing proposal mechanism riding it-A -- renter-confirmation material, zero movement in field_state, zero wrong possible.

items.title: uploads 31 image fields: unmeasurable by the loop's protected instruments
fields: see loop/baseline.json field_state, the 31 uploads entries valued image_only
items.root_cause_note: scripts/measure_label_mapping._tally skips rasterized documents outright, and loop/tools/build_field_state.py reconciles against it by construction, so no change to core/ or ocr/ can ever flip these 31 in field_state. The measuring script is a protected instrument (G6); acting on this requires the instrument owner, not the loop -- the T5 precedent. The PRODUCT path is not blocked: api/upload.py already routes empty-text-layer uploads to ocr/ocr_extract.py and the uploads manifest records its measured behaviour, including the conf-based certainty='high' (design section 1g) that the owner should cap to 'low' as an ordinary reviewed change.
items.design_note: Design section 2 and C.4. Belongs in STOP.md alongside T5 at loop end.
